Statement Name: Persistent identifier page
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): ISO 24619:2011, ISO 26324:2022, ISO 690:2021 or any other relevant standard

Statement Name: Internationally recognized product ID
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Internationally recognized product IDs identification systems should be used, such as ISO/IEC 15459-4:2014

Statement Name: Production site code type in accordance with any internationally recognized system
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): ISO/IEC 6523-1 or other site identification systems as appropriate (ex. GLN)

Statement Name: Threshold at which the product composition is disclosed
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Internationally recognized systems such as the CAS Registry Number® should be used when reporting substances' names and identifiers. The details of the composition can be identified through various methods including IEC 824 74-1 or IEC 62474

Module: Hazardous substances and substances of concern
Statement Name: This product contains no known hazardous substances according to the relevant cited reference standards or regulations
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): The standard or regulation should apply to the target market and be publicly available, and shall be cited.

Statement Name: A declaration of hazardous substances in the product according to the selected relevant cited reference standards or regulations, is available publicly
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Regulation (EC) 1907/2006 (REACH), IEC 62474-database (ROHS), IEC 63000, IEC 62321

Statement Name: The pre-consumer and post-consumer recycled content is attributed recycled content, excluding book and claim model
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): If a chain of custody model is used (ex. Mass Balance), it should be cited and be in accordance with ISO 22095.

Statement Name: Mass fraction of renewable materials out of the total product mass
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): The specification for renewable material provided in ISO 59020:2024, A.2.4, is used to assess renewable materials. If a chain of custody model is used, such as Mass Blance, but excluding book and claim, it should be cited and in accordance with ISO 22095.

Statement Name: Fraction of energy that is renewable used in the production
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): An example of a standard that can provide frameworks for renewable energy assessment is ISO/IEC 13273-2:2015

Statement Name: Volume fraction of reused or recirculated water used in the production of the product
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): An example of a standard that can provide frameworks for reused or recirculated water assessment is ISO 59020:2024, Clause A.5, and ISO 14046

Statement Name: Product meeting relevant industrial state of the art reliability levels
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Reliability test methods and thresholds should be chosen based on the type, characteristics, intended use and operating conditions of the product. Examples of standards that can provide frameworks for reliability assessment are IEC 62308:2006 (for the electrotechnical sector) and EN 45552:2020 (for energy-related products).

Statement Name: Repairability of the product
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): This is described in EN 45554:2020, Clause 7 (for energy-related products)

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be updated to extend its useful life
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): An example of a standard that can provide frameworks on upgradability assessment is EN 45554

Statement Name: Ability of product to be disassembled
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): See ISO 8887-1 and EN 45556

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be reused as is
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): An example of a standard that can provide frameworks on reuse assessment is EN 45554

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be refurbished
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): An example of a standard that can provide frameworks on refurbishing assessment is EN 45554

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be dismantled
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Examples of a metric to count the steps of dismantling can be found in EN 45554:2020, Clause A.2

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be remanufactured
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Examples of standards that can provide frameworks on remanufacturing are ANSI/RIC001.2-2021, DIN SPEC 91472 or EN 45553:2020

Statement Name: The product is designed for cycling in the technical cycle
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): As defined in ISO 59004:2024, 3.1.20 annd using methods or frameworks that are in standards such as : ISO 59020:2024, A.3.4 and B.3.3; ISO 15270:2008; IEC/TR 62635:2012; EN 45555:2019; ISO 20915:2018.

Statement Name: Ability of the product to be recycled at a similar level of quality to the original input materials listed under product composition
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): A quality ratio for recycling material is proposed in the European Product Environmental Footprint

Statement Name: The product is designed for cycling in the biological cycle
Reference standard(s) or regulation(s): Refer to ISO 59020:2024, A.3.5. Various International Standards define industrial, home composting and biodigestion, and should be referenced, such as ISO 15270, ISO 17088 or ASTM D6400
Standards that can provide a framework on environmental benefits assessment such as ISO 59020:2024
